steps.tool_result:
{"ok": true, "tool_used": "regex", "suspicious_summary": "# Pre-filtered suspicious candidates (external/public + state-changing + no obvious access-control modifier):
- `initialize` (visibility=external, modifiers=initializer)
- `pledgeAndBorrow` (visibility=external, modifiers=nonReentrant)
- `auction` (visibility=external, modifiers=nonReentrant, whenNotPaused)
- `withdrawNFT` (visibility=external, modifiers=nonReentrant, whenNotPaused)
- `getOrderDetail` (visibility=external, modifiers=address, collection, uint256, tokenId, address, pledger)
- `notifyOrderLiquidated` (visibility=external, modifiers=none)
- `notifyRepayBorrow` (visibility=external, modifiers=none)
- `isOrderLiquidated` (visibility=public, modifiers=bool)
- `getOrderState` (visibility=external, modifiers=OrderState, orderState)
- `airDrop` (visibility=public, modifiers=none)

The ground-truth vulnerable function is highly likely to be in the list above. Use this as your primary candidate pool when ranking top-3."}

tool_args.hypothesis: setController has no access control so anyone can set controller to attacker address, then call notifyOrderLiquidated (restricted to controller) to liquidate/withdraw arbitrary NFTs.

steps.tool_result: {"ok": true, "target_function": "setController", "hypothesis": "setController has no access control so anyone can set controller to attacker address, then call notifyOrderLiquidated (restricted to controller) to liquidate/withdraw arbitrary NFTs."}

tool_args.exploit_logic: Call setController from attacker to point controller to attacker-controlled contract; create a fake controller that calls notifyOrderLiquidated on XNFT to mark an order liquidated; if pledger equals liquidator path, attacker can force withdraw NFT to themselves. The PoC will: 1) deploy XNFT and initialize; 2) deploy a minimal malicious controller that calls notifyOrderLiquidated with chosen orderId/liquidator/liquidatedPrice/xToken; 3) setController to malicious controller; 4) create an order in XNFT by calling pledgeInternal via pledge (simulate NFT by using a dummy ERC721 and transfer to contract); 5) call malicious controller to trigger notifyOrderLiquidated and assert the NFT has been transferred to attacker.
